stem: A industrial engineering study measures the assembly time (seconds) for a sample of 20 factory workers. The sample mean is 158.6 sec with a sample standard deviation of 28.0 sec. However, 3 of the 20 observations appear to be outliers (values more than 2.5 standard deviations from the mean). If these outliers are removed, the remaining 17 observations have a mean of 153.1 sec and standard deviation of 21.0 sec. Estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval that accounts for the uncertainty about whether outliers should be included.

ground_truth.answer: With all data: mean = 158.6, 95% CI = [145.5, 171.7]. Without outliers: mean = 153.1, 95% CI = [142.9, 163.3]. Combined interval accounting for analytic uncertainty: [142.9, 171.7]. Point estimate: 155.8 sec.

solution_steps: The outlier treatment choice creates structural uncertainty. Combined interval: [142.9, 171.7].
common_failure_modes: Using only the full sample without considering outlier impact
Removing outliers without reporting both analyses
Not recognizing that the choice of analysis method is itself a source of uncertainty
